verdict.answer_first: Tie: both state the command immediately: "On macOS: lsof -ti :8080 | xargs kill".
verdict.decisiveness: A is more decisive: it gives the main command, then only the necessary force option; B adds a small option survey with "Quick variants" and "Want me to run it?".
verdict.fabrication: B likely invents/overclaims: "avoids xargs erroring on no match" is attached to kill $(...) even though that can run kill with no args; A says "xargs runs no command" which may depend on xargs behavior, but is presented as harmless.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it gives the command, the force fallback, and a check command without extra distracting variants.
